pullRequest.title: DOC Add usage example for mean_prediction in advanced MetricFrame guide
pullRequest.body:
Closes #1475

Adds a usage example for `mean_prediction` in the Advanced MetricFrame section of the User Guide.

Noticed this while working on a churn model. The example shows how `mean_prediction` can be used with `MetricFrame` to compare predicted values across groups.

Tests:
- [x] no new tests required

Documentation:
- [x] user guide added or updated

pullRequest.title: FIX Bug in the callback validation of AdversarialFairnessClassifier
pullRequest.body:
<!--- If relevant, make sure to add a description of your changes in docs/user_guide/installation_and_version_guide/v<major>.<minor>.<patch>.rst -->

## Description
Fixes #1537.

There is a larger issue that causes this problem, as currently data validation and transformation are combined in one step. This gets the data in the validation function and one-hot encodes it before continuing with the fitting, and we then have a very different representation in the middle step to compare with the predictions, which go through the inverse transform in before the `predict` function outputs them. 

I am not sure of the computational impact of having to do this transformation each time. It would definitely be more optimal to break down the `_validate_input` function and refactor parts of it, but this fixes the bug for now.

There was a more intricate way of dealing with the `y` types and conversations before we simplified that, and I am a bit uncomfortable with the code quality/integrity state of the whole module as it is, and if it works as intended. I was really new to the project when I worked on making the module more `sklearn` compatible, and I remember a lot of issues, but it was difficult for me to make sense of it all without depending heavily on reviews at the time. 

There are multiple issues to work on the module in more detail at the moment:
- #1480
- #1419
- https://github.com/fairlearn/fairlearn/pull/1529#discussion_r1998613908

This is the output atm, early stopping is working again too: 
```
Output from /Users/tamara/FOSS/fairlearn/examples/plot_adversarial_fine_tuning.py
DP diff: 0.0003, accuracy: 0.6146, selection_rate: 0.5991
DP diff: 0.0000, accuracy: 0.7964, selection_rate: 0.0520
DP diff: 0.0035, accuracy: 0.8088, selection_rate: 0.0904
Output from /Users/tamara/FOSS/fairlearn/examples/plot_adversarial_fine_tuning.py
DP diff: 0.0334, accuracy: 0.8061, selection_rate: 0.9093
        accuracy  selection_rate
sex
Female  0.910637        0.931664
Male    0.754399        0.898240
```

cc @hildeweerts for review

pullRequest.title: MNT Remove Pillow deprecation warning by requiring matplotlib>=3.10.5
pullRequest.body:
### Description
This PR resolves issue #1586 by fixing the root cause of the Pillow deprecation warning rather than just suppressing it.
The warning ('mode' parameter is deprecated and will be removed in Pillow 13) originates from matplotlib's internal code when it calls Image.fromarray() with the mode parameter. This was fixed upstream in matplotlib v3.10.5.

**Changes made:**
Added matplotlib>=3.10.5 to requirements-dev.txt
Added matplotlib==3.10.5 to requirements-min.txt
Removed the warning suppression filter from pyproject.toml
Fixes #1586.

**Tests**
no new tests required

**Documentation**
no documentation changes needed,  considering that it was a dependency version issue

pullRequest.title: DOC: Fix true positive rate formulation in equal opportunity metrics
pullRequest.body:
- Change E[h(X) | A=a] to P[h(X)=1 | A=a, Y=1] in equal_opportunity_difference
- Change E[h(X) | A=a] to P[h(X)=1 | A=a, Y=1] in equal_opportunity_ratio
- Fixes #1594

<!--- If relevant, make sure to add a description of your changes in docs/user_guide/installation_and_version_guide/v<major>.<minor>.<patch>.rst -->

## Description
<!--- Provide a general summary of your changes. -->
Fix the true positive rate formulation in equal opportunity metrics documentation. The docstrings incorrectly defined true positive rate as E[h(X) | A=a] when it should be P[h(X)=1 | A=a, Y=1].

<!--- Mention related issues, pull requests, or discussions with #<issue/PR/discussion ID>. -->
Fixes #1594
